result:
The spec is fully implemented and the project builds cleanly with 0 warnings and 0 errors, well within budget (4 build cycles used).

I added three functions to `CsvRow.calr` in the Calor DSL, following the conventions of the existing `NeedsQuoting` fixture (same `§F{fNNN:Name:pub} (type:arg) -> ret` inline-signature style, s-expression bodies, indentation-based blocks):

- **`EscapeField`** (`f002`) — calls `NeedsQuoting`; if true, doubles internal quotes via `replace` and wraps in quotes, otherwise returns the field verbatim.
- **`JoinRow`** (`f003`) — folds over the list with `§EACH`, escaping each field and inserting commas between them; an empty list yields the empty string.
- **`SplitRow`** (`f004`) — an index-based state machine: quoted fields consume until an un-doubled quote (doubled quotes contribute one literal quote, commas/CR/LF inside are content), unquoted fields end at the next comma or end of line, and a trailing comma produces a final empty field. The empty line parses to a single empty field.

Two Calor-specific issues surfaced during the build cycles: an empty `§LIST` needs an explicit `§/LIST` closer when the next statement sits at the same indent, and `§PUSH` on the local result list requires declaring the `mut` effect (`§E{mut}`) — that's still pure computation externally (local mutation only, no I/O or global state), which the effects checker enforces.

Beyond the clean build, I verified behavior with a throwaway console project (created in /tmp, now deleted — no test files added to the source tree): round-trip `SplitRow(JoinRow(fields))` across edge cases (embedded commas/quotes/CR/LF, empty fields, trailing commas, quote-only fields) plus the `EscapeField` and empty-list/empty-line rules from the spec — all passed.
